$251M has gone into agent security on one bet: put the perimeter between intent and tool call.  Capsule Security ($7M, ex-Unit 8200 team, CVE disclosures against Microsoft Copilot Studio). Runlayer's ToolGuard. The ~$40M MCP-security cohort.  The bet is correct. It's also incomplete.  The checkpoint primitive ends when the tool returns. Memory poisoning doesn't end there: MITRE ATLAS AML.T0080, OWASP LLM01, >95% injection success in MINJA research against production agents. The exploit runs once. The memory runs indefinitely.  That points to a different architectural location: trust-gated writes, hash-chain audit, per-agent provenance. None of it extends from a tool-call checkpoint without rearchitecting.  Operant AI markets memory-poisoning coverage. The mechanism is read-path surveillance, not write-time certification. Positioning extension, not architectural extension. Here's how it works: — Fake recruiter contacts you on LinkedIn — Offers a legit-looking Solidity assessment — Repo contains a malicious npm package: chain-await-test — Running npm install executes a hidden payload that phones home and exfiltrates your system data The package pretends to be a test utility but contains: — axios + request (to send your data out) — sqlite3 (to harvest and store data) — A remote payload fetched from jsonkeeper dot com — Obfuscated code designed to steal wallet keys and passwords.  I caught it in time. My cold storage was never at risk. But someone less paranoid could lose everything.  Before you run ANY coding assessment: ✅ Check package.json for suspicious dependencies ✅ Never run npm install on unknown repos without reviewing first ✅ Keep seed phrases offline always ✅ Never connect your wallet during an "interview" Report suspicious packages to security@npmjs.com @FBI  The GitHub account was PropVision26318 — report it. cURL 개발자는 앤트로픽의 '버그 사냥' 신화는 역사상 최고의 마케팅 전략이었다고 말했습니다.  cURL 개발자 다니엘 스텐버그는 앤트로픽의 과장된 버그 탐지 모델인 미토스(Mythos) 가 cURL에서 심각도가 낮은 취약점 하나와 몇 가지 비보안 버그만 발견했으며, 이는 그가 예상했던 것보다 훨씬 적은 수치라고 밝혔습니다. 그는 미토스가 유용할 수는 있지만, 다른 최신 AI 코드 분석 도구보다 의미 있게 뛰어난 것은 아니라고 주장합니다. 스텐버그는 자신의 블로그 게시글에서 "개인적으로 내린 결론은 이 모델에 대한 지금까지의 과도한 홍보가 주로 마케팅에 불과했다는 것입니다."라고 말했습니다. "미토스가 기존의 다른 도구들보다 특별히 더 높은 수준이나 더 정교한 방식으로 문제를 찾아낸다는 증거는 찾을 수 없습니다." 그는 미토스를 "확실히 놀랍도록 성공적인 마케팅 전략"이라고 혹평했습니다. 더 레지스터(The Register)는 다음과 같이 보도했습니다.  it.slashdot.org/story/26/05/…

sample_tweets.text: 🇯🇵 일본 3대 메가뱅크, 이달 말 Anthropic Mythos 접근 예정  오늘의 Passive 섹터 모멘텀 탭: 소프트웨어 IGV 1개월 강세. AI 사이버 보안 수요가 금융 섹터로 확산되는 구간. 📊 국내주식/미국주식 분석 사이트 👉 링크는 프로필에 있습니다  Reuters와 Bloomberg 보도에 따르면 MUFG, Mizuho, SMFG 일본 3대 메가뱅크가 이달 말까지 Anthropic의 Mythos 모델에 접근할 예정이다. 일본 기업이 Mythos에 접근하는 것은 이번이 처음이다.   이 결정은 스콧 베센트 미국 재무장관이 도쿄에서 일본 재무장관과 면담하는 자리에서 전달된 것으로 알려졌다. Mythos 접근권이 상업 채널이 아닌 미국 재무부 외교 채널을 통해 전달된 구조다.   Mythos가 무엇인지 맥락이 중요하다.  Mythos는 방어적 사이버 보안 목적으로 설계된 AI 모델이다. 내부 테스트에서 모든 주요 운영체제와 웹 브라우저에 걸쳐 수천 개의 미발견 취약점을 찾아냈고, Firefox는 단 한 번의 Mythos 평가 후 271개의 취약점을 패치했다. Anthropic은 이를 "사이버 위험의 순간"으로 규정하며 접근을 엄격히 통제하고 있다.   Anthropic의 Project Glasswing 프레임워크 아래 현재 AWS, Apple, Cisco, Google, JPMorganChase, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palo Alto Networks 등 12개 파트너와 약 40개 기관만이 접근권을 보유하고 있다.   이번 발표가 단순한 AI 서비스 확장이 아닌 이유가 있다.  유럽 국가들은 미국이 접근 파트너 확장을 차단하고 있다고 불만을 표명했고, 지난주 에코핀 회의에서 EU 재무장관들이 이를 공식 제기했다. 미국 재무부가 Mythos 접근권을 일본에 먼저 제공한 것은 기술 외교의 새로운 형태로 읽힌다.  일본 재무장관 카타야마는 Mythos 관련 사이버 리스크를 다루기 위한 36개 공공·민간 기관 합동 워킹그룹을 구성한다고 발표했다. 일본은행과 3대 메가뱅크, Anthropic·OpenAI 일본법인이 참여한다.   오늘의 Passive 시장 밸류 탭: S&P 500 PER 27.73배. AI 보안 기대가 밸류에이션에 반영되는 흐름.  📊 Passive: 국내주식/미국주식 분석 플랫폼 👉 링크는 프로필에 있습니다  #미국주식 #Anthropic #Mythos #일본 #MUFG #Mizuho #SMFG #AI #사이버보안 #금융 #Passive

🔒 Private DFIR Report: ViewState of Mind: Gladinet Exploit Opens the Door  In January, we observed a threat actor gain initial access to an environment by exploiting CVE-2025-30406 on an exposed Gladinet CentreStack server. Looking at the network traffic at the time of this connection showed large VIEWSTATE payloads being sent to the server. Based on this pattern in the network traffic, the command execution from the IIS server, and the version of Gladinet CentreStack, we assessed that the threat actor successfully exploited CVE-2025-30406 for initial access in this intrusion. New hands-on DFIR lab teaches persistence mechanism hunting in Elastic SIEM using realistic CartFlow Commerce breach scenario. Three-week attacker dwell time with registry run keys, scheduled tasks, and startup folder redundancy detected through ES|QL correlation.  Detailed breakdown: • Covers MITRE T1547.001 (Registry Run Keys), T1053.005 (Scheduled Tasks), T1060 (Startup Folder) persistence techniques • Lab uses 30-day persistence-lab-logs dataset with buried attacker activity across developer workstations • Attack timeline: Registry run key April 7th 2AM → scheduled task April 11th → startup folder April 18th • ES|QL queries detect off-hours registry writes to HKCU\Run pointing to AppData binaries, shell-spawned schtasks.exe with SYSTEM privileges, executable drops to Windows Startup folders  Hunt patterns include: • Time-based analysis: `DATE_EXTRACT("HOUR_OF_DAY", @timestamp)` flags 2AM-5AM activity • Path risk scoring: AppData/ProgramData binary locations vs legitimate Program Files • Process ancestry: cmd.exe/powershell.exe spawning schtasks.exe indicates manual attacker activity • Campaign scope correlation: `COUNT_DISTINCT(mechanism)` across hosts reveals redundant persistence deployment  Complete with Sigma detection rule conversion and GitHub portfolio integration. Hunt notebook templates track IOCs, execution timelines, and affected host risk scoring for incident response prioritization.  #DFIR_Radar

🚨Tonight’s update: Three women found dead off Brighton beach as police launch major investigation.  Three Women Found Dead Off Brighton Beach as Police Launch Sensitive Investigation.  The incident triggered a large emergency response at around 5.45am, when crews were called to reports of concern for a woman in the water near the Black Rocks car park on Madeira Drive. On arrival, officers recovered her body — and soon after, two more women were found dead nearby.  The beach, which was cordoned off for much of the morning, has since reopened. Officers have now left the scene following what police described as a “significant operation”.  Detectives say their immediate priority is to identify the three women and trace their families, who are not yet aware of the deaths.  Chief Superintendent Adam Hays acknowledged the shock felt locally and urged the public to avoid speculation while enquiries continue.  Officers are currently pursuing multiple lines of enquiry to establish how the women came to be in the water and whether the deaths are linked.  Police are asking anyone who may have seen something unusual in the area overnight, or who has information that could help, to come forward.  Members of the public can contact officers online or via 101, quoting Operation Ledmore.

Why the International Community Treats North Korea as a Manageable Threat Rather Than Seeking to Eliminate It.  North Korea has long been regarded as one of the most repressive regimes in the world, with a record of nuclear development, human rights violations, and threats to neighboring countries.  In a recent analysis, a commentator explores why the global community, despite acknowledging the severity of the Kim Jong-un regime, continues to manage rather than eliminate the threat.  The reasoning is rooted in realpolitik: removing Kim could trigger unpredictable chaos, including a power struggle that risks rogue nuclear weapons, whereas a predictable dictator can be contained through deterrence and diplomacy.  This approach is contrasted with past interventions like the 2003 Iraq war, where the regional risk was considered lower.  For South Korea, which shares a border with North Korea and relies on the US alliance for security, these global calculations have direct implications for national defense strategy, diplomatic policy, and public safety.  In an era of complex geopolitics, understanding why certain regimes persist despite widespread condemnation is essential for citizens to evaluate the effectiveness of international security frameworks and their own country’s position within them.  Such insights help the public distinguish between moral rhetoric and strategic reality in foreign policy.  Do you believe the international community should pursue more decisive action to address the North Korean regime, or is the current strategy of careful management the only realistic option given the risks involved?

The leaked information allegedly includes a broad range of corporate, financial, and internal system records, such as:  - Customer and company names - Internal reference numbers and short codes - Trading addresses and postal codes - Phone numbers, fax numbers, and email addresses - Website URLs - Credit limits and payment-related information - VAT registration numbers - SIC codes, DUNS numbers, and company registration details - Employee counts and industry classifications - Operational records including account opening/amendment dates - Legacy system identifiers and division/source codes  The actor also claims the dataset contains internal employee login information, including staff email addresses, names, and MD5 password hashes.  According to the post, the allegedly affected entities include organizations across financial services, insurance, energy, and media sectors, with references to institutions such as Bank of Scotland, HBOS, and Halifax appearing within the dataset.  If authentic, the exposed information could facilitate targeted phishing, business email compromise (BEC), credential attacks, and fraud operations against both employees and partner organizations. sample_tweets.text: For the first time, Daily Dark Web interviewed individuals claiming to be connected to the ShinyHunters ecosystem.  Below are selected excerpts from the interview.  Q: After multiple arrests and increased pressure, how much fragmentation occurred inside the ecosystem?  A: “Trust deteriorated, communication channels became unstable, and many individuals either disappeared, rebranded, or moved into smaller private circles. These ecosystems rarely survive major disruption without internal instability.”  Q: Why did the ShinyHunters identity become so influential?  A: “A recognized name creates visibility, credibility, influence, and psychological leverage. Established underground brands carry enormous reputation value.”  Q: How important are cryptographic identities and reputation systems inside underground ecosystems?  A: “PGP keys, historical handles, and reputation systems function as substitutes for legal trust. Reputation becomes a form of currency.”  Q: Do underground ecosystems have a trust problem?  A: “Absolutely. Reputation-based systems remain vulnerable to deception, impersonation, and identity abuse.”  Q: How common are internal conflicts and betrayal?  A: “Internal betrayal, financial disputes, revenge leaks, and personal conflicts are extremely common. These environments are often unstable alliances driven by opportunism and distrust.”  Q: How common is impersonation inside cybercrime ecosystems?  A: “Impersonation happens constantly. Some actors imitate known identities or attempt to inherit abandoned brands to gain credibility and visibility.”  Q: Are these groups highly organized?  A: “Most operations are far less organized than the public imagines. They are often loose networks of temporary collaborators rather than rigid organizations.”  Q: How interconnected are underground ecosystems today?  A: “Forums, encrypted chats, marketplaces, and private communities overlap extensively. Information and reputation move quickly between these spaces.”  Q: Did media attention contribute to the growth of the brand?  A: “Once a name becomes associated with high-profile incidents, the brand itself acquires power beyond the individuals originally connected to it.”  Q: What are the biggest operational security failures inside these ecosystems?  A: “The biggest failures are usually human rather than technical — ego, overconfidence, poor compartmentalization, and trusting the wrong people.”  The full 20-question interview is available exclusively for DDW subscribers.  Disclaimer: This interview is shared strictly for journalistic, research, and cybersecurity awareness purposes. Statements made by interview subjects have not been independently verified.  #DDW #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec

This website is like a piece of paper that's glued to the wall like you ripped it off of a magazine. There's panning and scrolling. The mental model of the page is vividly clear. Navigation is straightforward and predictable. Menu doesn't move. The 'staticness' of these old sites is undervalued and now kind of forgotten.  Compare this with modern full screen webapps that give you no sense of where you are on the page and molasses-like animated UI that captures native scrolling. These websites have their own abstraction layer to navigate, browse and present things. Almost like motion picture that presents information to you and you don't have much control. Signifiers that visually inform you of interactability are misplaced or entirely missing. You have some illusion of control but it's nowhere as explicit as a static page.  There is an ad-tech-smell and "pop" in modern websites as they take over your visual field in mysterious, unpredictable ways. Doesn't let go of the attention easily. Huge typography and motion effects captivate users kinda like a 30-sec advertisement. Crack cocaine of information consumption.  Combine this with terrible decisions at the OS-level like making scrollbars invisible, practice of UI/UX is deeply unserious today. It's not like we don't have solutions and it's an unknown problem. We have the blueprints. We had it all and we deliberately abandoned it with great carelessness. But in a certain light, the practice of UI/UX today is deeply serious in the ad-tech aspects and bedazzles their userbase with ever-increasing sense of bedazzlement. What purpose does Apple Liquid Glass UI serve otherwise?  Open for discussion and critique.

It is hard to understate how much more hardened Apple's Application-Processor-side of WiFi is than any other operating system out there. Between MIE and the XZM allocator there's some serious hardening on the latest iOS and iPhone 17.  We spend a lot of time in wifi land and Apple's the gold standard here.  The first big thing to know about Apple's WiFi on iOS is that they removed attack surface from the kernel and brought it into userland with DriverKit (developer.apple.com/videos/p…). The concept was initially formed by Simon Douglas while he was at NeXT, Inc working for Steve Jobs and brought to Mac in 2019 by Douglas and team. Most memory corruption can't get far by design and it should be exceedingly difficult to see another Ian Beer type wifi exploit (projectzero.google/2020/12/a…)  This use after free bug occured in `wifid`, a root userland process on iOS and can be triggered without any user interaction.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-05-14 08:06 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 보안 업계는 AI 모델(Anthropic의 Claude Mythos, OpenAI의 GPT-5.5 등)을 활용한 자동화된 취약점 탐색 및 공격 체인 구성이 급격히 고도화되는 'Mythos Moment'를 맞이하고 있습니다. 랜섬웨어 그룹은 단순 암호화에서 데이터 탈취 및 유출(Data Exfiltration) 위주로 전략을 수정하고 있으며, 특히 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)과 오픈소스 패키지(RubyGems 등)를 악용한 데이터 유출 시도가 빈번합니다. 또한, Linux 커널 취약점(Copy Fail, Dirty Frag)이 연이어 발견되어 인프라 단의 보안 패치와 런타임 모니터링의 중요성이 극대화되었습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Nitrogen (SecurityWeek/BleepingComputer):** Foxconn 북미 공장을 타겟으로 8TB 데이터를 탈취하고 랜섬웨어 공격 수행.
*   **MuddyWater (BleepingComputer):** 한국 전자제품 제조사를 포함한 다수의 글로벌 기업을 대상으로 한 사이버 스파이 캠페인 전개.
*   **The Gentlemen (Check Point Research):** RaaS(Ransomware-as-a-Service) 모델을 운영하며 공격적인 TTPs를 사용하는 신흥 랜섬웨어 그룹.
*   **UNC6692 (Mandiant):** 사회공학적 기법을 활용한 다단계 침입 및 커스텀 악성코드 배포.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **MuddyWater 캠페인:** 이란 연계 해킹 그룹인 MuddyWater가 한국의 주요 전자제품 제조사를 포함한 9개 이상의 글로벌 조직을 대상으로 사이버 스파이 활동을 수행 중임이 확인되었습니다.
*   **피싱 메일 주의:** 이스트시큐리티(ESRC)에 따르면 국세청 및 금융기관을 사칭한 PhaaS(Phishing-as-a-Service) 기반의 기업 타겟형 피싱 메일이 국내에서 포착되었습니다.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-31431 (Copy Fail):** Linux Kernel LPE, root 권한 탈취 가능, 매우 심각.
*   **CVE-2026-43284 (Dirty Frag):** Linux Kernel LPE, root 권한 탈취 가능, 심각.
*   **CVE-2026-0300:** PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal, RCE 가능, 심각.
*   **CVE-2025-68670:** xrdp server component, pre-auth RCE, 심각.
*   **CVE-2026-45185:** Exim mailer, GnuTLS/BDAT traffic을 통한 RCE 가능, 심각.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **해시:** `D980E12C027180B2F4BDF96405E7544E` (OverlordRAT) ([Twitter/trinkoX](https://twitter.com/trinkoX/status/2054695757114937456#m))
*   **CVE:**
    *   `CVE-2026-31431` ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cve-2026-31431-copy-fail/))
    *   `CVE-2026-43284` ([Qualys](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research))
    *   `CVE-2026-0300` ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/captive-portal-zero-day/))
    *   `CVE-2025-68670` ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/cve-2025-68670/119742/))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Linux 시스템 패치:** Copy Fail(CVE-2026-31431) 및 Dirty Frag(CVE-2026-43284)에 대한 최신 커널 업데이트를 즉시 적용하십시오.
*   **PAN-OS 보안 점검:** Palo Alto Networks 장비의 Captive Portal(CVE-2026-0300) 취약점 패치 여부를 확인하고, 노출된 포트의 접근 제어를 강화하십시오.
*   **피싱 대응 강화:** 국세청 및 금융기관 사칭 메일에 대한 임직원 교육을 강화하고, 이메일 보안 솔루션의 PhaaS 탐지 정책을 업데이트하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8/10
*   **근거:** Linux 커널 취약점과 AI를 활용한 자동화된 공격이 결합되어 공격 성공률이 높아졌으며, 대규모 제조사 및 인프라를 타겟으로 한 랜섬웨어 활동이 활발함.
